name: VMware CloudHealth FinOps Platform API
what_it_does: Multi-cloud FinOps and governance platform with REST API — enabling FinOps teams to analyze cloud costs across AWS, Azure, GCP, and Kubernetes, enforce cost policies, manage reserved instances/commitments, generate chargeback reports, and monitor cloud spending trends, acquired by VMware in 2018 and now part of Broadcom's portfolio.
use_cases: Analyzing multi-cloud costs via CloudHealth API for unified AWS/Azure/GCP spend visibility
Generating chargeback reports via CloudHealth API for team and cost center allocation
Monitoring reserved instance utilization via CloudHealth API for commitment optimization
Enforcing cost governance policies via CloudHealth API for cloud budget compliance
Exporting FinOps data via CloudHealth API for executive dashboards and cost reporting
not_for: Kubernetes-only cost management (use Kubecost/OpenCost for K8s-specific)
Real-time cloud monitoring and alerting (use Datadog/CloudWatch for operational monitoring)
Small teams with single-cloud simple setups (CloudHealth is enterprise multi-cloud)
best_when: A large enterprise FinOps team needs multi-cloud cost visibility, chargeback, and governance across AWS, Azure, and GCP with enterprise features like reserved instance management.
avoid_when: You're a small/mid-size team, single-cloud, or need Kubernetes-specific cost allocation without broader cloud context.

auth.notes: API key via api_key query parameter or Authorization: Bearer header. API key from CloudHealth account settings. REST API at chapi.cloudhealthtech.com/v1/. No OAuth2. Tenant-specific API key (multi-tenant customers: separate key per tenant). AWS/Azure/GCP cloud account credentials managed in CloudHealth UI. No webhooks.

pricing.paid_tiers: Custom enterprise pricing; estimated $30-50k/year for mid-enterprise
pricing.notes: US company (Boston). Founded 2012, acquired by VMware 2018 for $500M. Now part of Broadcom (acquired VMware 2023). Broadcom acquisition of VMware: significant product portfolio changes ongoing — verify CloudHealth roadmap. Products: Cost, Governance, Security, Sustainability. Integrations: AWS Cost and Usage Report, Azure Cost Management, GCP Billing, Kubernetes. AWS resale: available via AWS Marketplace. Reserved Instance Optimizer: rightsizing and commitment recommendations. Flex accounts: multi-account AWS management. Used by: large enterprises, MSPs. Ownership chain: CloudHealth → VMware → Broadcom.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; enterprise rate limits enforced

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API key in query param OR Bearer. Broadcom ownership (VMware acquisition). US data residency. Multi-cloud (AWS/Azure/GCP). No webhooks. Reserved instance management. Chargeback reports. Enterprise-only pricing.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All cost data reads are idempotent — historical billing data is deterministic. Report generation: idempotent by date range and filter parameters.
known_agent_gotchas: Broadcom acquired VMware (2023): CloudHealth product direction uncertain — verify roadmap before multi-year commitment
API key in query parameter: sensitive — prefer Authorization: Bearer header to avoid URL logging
Multi-tenant: separate API keys per CloudHealth tenant — verify which tenant key to use
Cloud cost data is 24-48 hours delayed: AWS/Azure/GCP billing APIs have inherent latency
RI management: reserved instance and savings plan analysis requires cloud billing integration setup
US data residency only — verify GDPR compliance for EU organizations processing cost data

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. API key in query param (logging risk) — prefer Bearer header. SOC2 certified. Cloud cost data is financially sensitive. Broadcom ownership: supply chain risk from PE-backed acquirer. AWS/Azure/GCP credentials stored in CloudHealth — significant trust surface. US data residency. GDPR compliance via DPA. No OAuth2 for fine-grained access. Multi-cloud cloud account credentials: high blast radius if compromised.
